rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions in component type scopes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE annex blocks within valid AADL component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE expressions using visible identifiers and proper type alignment.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should use visible ports and constants; implementation bindings go in implementation scopes.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid AADL component features and types.
Avoid invented numeric constraints; only include bounds explicitly stated in requirements.
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE variables unless typed ports or constants are exposed.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `eq` for boolean expressions and `const` for named limits in component type scopes.
Ensure type consistency between AGREE declarations and AADL port types.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not assume numeric bounds for ports without explicit requirement or AADL type support.
Do not treat event ports as numeric values in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints
Model fusion and architecture redesign patterns
